title: reason
description: These are all words for a cause or an explanation for something that has happened or that somebody has done.

definitions.definition: a cause or an explanation for something that has happened or that somebody has done; a fact that makes it right or fair to do something:

examples.en: He said no but he didn’t give a reason.
examples.cn: 他说不行，但没有说明原因。

definitions.definition: a statement, fact or situation that tells you why something has happened; a reason given for something:

examples.en: The most likely explanation is that his plane was delayed.
examples.cn: 最可能的解释是他的飞机晚点了。

examples.en: She left the room abruptly without explanation.
examples.cn: 她未作解释就突然离开了房间。

definitions.definition: (rather formal ) a good or true reason for saying, doing or believing something: 指说、做或相信某事的充分理由、根据：

examples.en: You have no grounds for complaint.

definitions.definition_cn: 指说、做或相信某事的充分理由、根据：

definitions.definition: (rather formal ) the reason why people take a particular action: 指原因、缘由：

examples.en: On what basis will this decision be made?
examples.cn: 将基于何种原因作出这一决定呢？

definitions.definition: a reason, either true or invented, that you give to explain or defend your behaviour; a good reason that you give for doing something that you want to do for other reasons:

examples.en: Late again! What’s your excuse this time?
examples.cn: 又迟到了！你这次有什么借口？

examples.en: It gave me an excuse to take the car.
examples.cn: 这使我有理由开车去了。

definitions.definition: a reason that explains somebody’s behaviour:

examples.en: There seemed to be no motive for the murder.
examples.cn: 这起谋杀案看不出有什么动机。

definitions.definition: (rather formal ) a good reason why something exists or is done: 指事物存在或做某事的正当理由：

examples.en: I can see no possible justification for any further tax increases.
examples.cn: 我看不出还能提出什么理由来加税了。

definitions.definition_cn: 指事物存在或做某事的正当理由：
